input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (previously used in .NET 9).

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` mode was deprecated. .NET MAUI has been evolving its approach to handle this.

In .NET 10 (which would be building on top of recent Android changes), the recommended approach is to use the `KeyboardAccelerators` or the new `Edge-to-Edge` mode handling. Actually, let me think about this more carefully.


The modern approach involves using the `WindowInsets` API on Android or leveraging the `AndroidX` WindowInsets approach to handle keyboard insets properly, since the older resize mode is no longer viable in newer Android versions. For .NET MAUI on Android, the core issue is that `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` behavior changed on Android 11+, so the practical workarounds are using `WindowCompat` APIs to manually handle keyboard insets with bottom padding, wrapping content in a `ScrollView` with `AdjustPan`, or leveraging community handlers and behaviors for this common problem.
